matches.rationale: Same Edit trigger and README-path prerequisite; the extracted path regex is narrower than all possible subdirectories but still targets the same action.
Same Write trigger and README-path prerequisite; the extracted path regex only enumerates major repository directories, which is partial coverage.
Directly targets edits to Next.js source or tests and requires starting the watch process first; it omits the explicit explain-if-skipped clause.
A git checkout is the extracted proxy for switching branches, and the evidence directly requires the full bootstrap build.
Same Write action: creating test files must go through pnpm new-test. The filename regex covers only conventional .test files, so coverage is partial.
Same Edit target: test waiting implemented with setTimeout, with evidence requiring retry() instead.
Same Edit target: use of deprecated check() in tests, with retry() plus expect() given as the replacement.
Same Edit target: an inline files object in test fixture configuration.
Targets gh PR creation or editing and requires the exact marker in the PR description.
Targets commands containing literal secret-like environment values; this covers the no-literal-secrets clause but omits the requirement to mirror CI names and modes.
Same Bash action: staging or committing local environment/secret files.
Targets git remote URL mutation and carries the same prohibition on HTTPS/authentication workarounds unless requested; the SSH-failure precondition is not encoded.
Same force-push or rebase action and SSH-preflight guidance; partial-clone state is represented only in the evidence.
Same git commit action and prohibition on generated-by/co-author attribution.
Same pre-commit action and requirement to run the documented formatting and lint validation.
Exact Bash action and prohibition: gh pr ready must be left to the user.
Targets the four mode-specific integration-test commands and requires rebuilding after source changes.
Targets feature-flag wiring through config-shared and carries the type, schema, and conditional define-env requirements; the trigger is narrower than all ways of adding a flag.
Same Edit target: react-server-dom-webpack imports outside entry-base.ts, with the same export-consumption alternative.
Targets require() edits in app-page.ts and prohibits the unresolvable relative internal-module form; the regex also catches non-relative requires.
Same test Edit action involving browser.back(); the router-act and visible-accordion conditions are retained only in evidence.
Same Rust Edit target and preference against new super:: imports, including the inline-mod exception in evidence.
Same documentation Edit target: adding highlight attributes and counting actual code-block lines.
Targets server-code header reads and carries the security-review requirement for nonstandard headers; the regex cannot distinguish standard headers or review context.

misses.note: The compiler emitted the watch prerequisite only for Edit, not the corresponding Write action for newly created source or integration-test files.
The keep-running instruction appears as a short comment inside the watch-build code block rather than as a command trigger.
The mode/bundler choice is expressed by a heading plus four commented commands in a code block, with no extracted Bash rule covering selection among them.
Compiler self-report: "Kill the background watch process when done iterating — cleanup reminder with no specific tool-call trigger."
Compiler self-report: "Prefer `pnpm --filter=next types` over a full build when only checking type errors — context-dependent preference, not a fixed action trigger."
This is phrased as a contextual preference after bootstrap, and no extracted rule targets the filtered core build.
No extracted Bash rule covers the compound full-build conditions; the checkout rule is reserved for the closer, explicit branch-switch rule g11.
Webpack selection is conveyed through two example commands in a code block, and no extracted Bash rule targets --webpack.
The prohibition is stated indirectly as the factual sentence that the flag does not exist.
The compiler represented mandatory test generation only as a Write guard; it emitted no Bash rule for invoking pnpm new-test.
The noninteractive invocation and argument format are embedded in code-block comments and an example command.

Compiler self-report: "Never re-run the same test suite with different grep filters; capture output once and analyze from file (repeated under Context-Efficient Workflows) — stateful/temporal guidance across multiple calls, not a single-call trigger."
Compiler self-report: "Run the pr-status script when the user asks about CI failures/PR reviews/status — triggered by user intent, not a tool call."
Compiler self-report: "Prioritize blocking failures first: build, lint, types, then tests (repeated under CI Analysis Tips) — general prioritization judgment, not action-bindable."
Compiler self-report: "Assume failures are real until disproven; treat 'Known Flaky Tests' as context, not auto-dismissal — general judgment call."

Compiler self-report: "Always match exact CI env vars when reproducing CI failures locally — too broad/contextual to bind without firing on every routine test run." The snapshot-only extraction is a different action.

Compiler self-report: "For module-resolution/build-graph fixes, use the normal mode-specific test command (repeated under Development Anti-Patterns) — advisory test-selection judgment, not a fixed trigger."
The actionable workflow is delegated to the referenced .agents/skills/pr-status-triage/SKILL.md, and no extracted Bash rule follows that reference.
Compiler self-report: "Prioritize CI failures over review comments — general prioritization, not action-bindable."

Compiler self-report groups this table-like nested-list recipe with others: "Common fast-check recipes (rust check/build → cargo fmt; lint/build → prettier --write) — conditioned on observing a specific CI failure type, not a static command trigger."

Compiler self-report groups this nested-list recipe with others: "Common fast-check recipes (rust check/build → cargo fmt; lint/build → prettier --write) — conditioned on observing a specific CI failure type, not a static command trigger."
The specific-test reproduction instruction is a nested CI-analysis list item, and no extracted Bash rule targets it.
The right-mode requirement is expressed by a heading and commented example commands rather than an extracted selection rule.
Compiler self-report: "Use `DEBUG=next:*` for debug logging / use `NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1` when testing locally — soft dev tips, not obligations tied to an action."
Compiler self-report: "Use `DEBUG=next:*` for debug logging / use `NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1` when testing locally — soft dev tips, not obligations tied to an action."
The NODE_ENV guidance is embedded in a comparison list under an environment-variable subsection, and no Edit rule targets that guard.
The __NEXT_DEV_SERVER guidance is embedded in a comparison list under an environment-variable subsection, and no Edit rule targets that guard.
This is a broad sensitivity principle rather than a syntactically identifiable command; the literal-secret extraction is reserved for the closer g39.
Compiler self-report: "Never print or paste secret values in chat responses, commits, or shared logs — not bindable to a specific tool call (chat responses aren't a tool)."
Compiler self-report: "If a required secret is missing locally, stop and ask the user rather than inventing placeholder credentials — judgment call on missing state, not a static trigger."
Compiler self-report: "Keep commit messages concise and descriptive — general style/tone."
Compiler self-report: "PR descriptions should focus on what changed and why — general style/tone."
The edge-build false-value requirement appears in a deep runtime/bundling list. The extracted define-env guardrail discusses user versus precompiled bundling and does not target this flag action.

unmatched_extracted.note: This is a real document rule for PR creation, distinct from gold g44's commit-only trigger.
This is real guidance about define-env.ts's user-bundling boundary, but no gold rule has that action/content; g51 is specifically about forcing edge flags false.
This is a real action-bindable rule requiring DCE-sensitive require() calls to remain behind compile-time if/else branches, absent from gold.
This is a real action-bindable rule specifically for snapshot updates under CI environment flags, absent from gold.
